scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a middle-office completion record tied to trade, account, LEI, SSI, and affirmation evidence before downstream handoff.
Missing account, LEI, and custodian setup correctly blocked action and triggered a prerequisite-focused intake disposition.
Conflicting SSI sources became an evidence-backed exception instead of an unsupported instruction choice.
Allocation and affirmation gaps prevented a false ready-to-settle claim and kept the work in an explicit rework state.
Completion without authoritative matching or affirmation evidence was rejected and held out of a false complete state.
Unsupported mandate and restricted-list overrides were refused with explicit control-boundary routing.
Generic onboarding and client-document work was rejected as adjacent scope rather than absorbed into middle-office ownership.
Corporate-action, collateral, and settlement-release requests correctly routed to more specialized lanes.
Realistic middle-office terminology and artifacts produced a lane-specific execution update rather than a generic operations note.
Conflicting SEC, custodian, and tenant guidance triggered escalation instead of unsupported synthesis.
Prime-broker and give-up readiness gaps became a documented setup blocker with the correct missing artifacts called out.
Tenant-specific market and asset-class differences stayed separated so adaptation preserved the public baseline instead of flattening it.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against a 0.92 minimum threshold for middle-office prerequisite validation, boundary safety, exception handling, and evidence-backed completion discipline.
Functionality coverage is complete across shared, workflow-phase, and cross-industry abilities.
